questions.text: How does Allah describe the legal status of the verses in Surah An-Nur?
questions.answer: As a Surah that He has sent down and made obligatory, containing clear signs so that people may remember.

questions.text: What is the legal punishment for Zina (adultery/fornication) for unmarried individuals as mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: One hundred lashes for each of them.

questions.text: Should believers feel pity for those being punished for Zina in a way that prevents them from carrying out the law?
questions.answer: No, if they believe in Allah and the Last Day, they should not let pity for them overcome them in the religion of Allah.

questions.text: Who should witness the carrying out of the punishment for Zina?
questions.answer: A group of the believers.

questions.text: What is the ruling regarding the marriage of those who commit Zina?
questions.answer: A male who commits Zina does not marry except a female who does the same or a polytheist, and vice versa; such a marriage is forbidden for the believers.

questions.text: What is the punishment for those who accuse chaste women without bringing four witnesses?
questions.answer: Eighty lashes, and their testimony is never to be accepted again.

questions.text: How are those who make false accusations against chaste women characterized?
questions.answer: They are the defiantly disobedient (Fasiqun), unless they repent and reform.

questions.text: What is 'Li'an' (Mutual Cursing) and when is it used?
questions.answer: A legal process where a husband accuses his wife of adultery without witnesses; they testify four times by Allah, with a fifth oath invoking Allah's curse if they are lying.

questions.text: How can a wife avert the punishment if her husband accuses her through Li'an?
questions.answer: She must testify four times by Allah that he is of the liars, and a fifth time that the wrath of Allah be upon her if he is of the truthful.

questions.text: Which major historical event is referred to as 'Al-Ifk' (The Slander) in this Surah?
questions.answer: The false accusation against Aisha (RA), the wife of the Prophet (SAW).

questions.text: Should the believers consider the slander incident as entirely bad for them?
questions.answer: No, Allah says: 'Do not consider it bad for you; rather it is good for you.' It served as a trial and purification for the community.

questions.text: What was the advice to the believers who first heard the slander against Aisha (RA)?
questions.answer: They should have thought well of their own people and said, 'This is a clear falsehood (Ifkun Mubin).'

questions.text: Why did Allah emphasize the need for 'four witnesses' regarding the slander?
questions.answer: To show that without four witnesses, those who make the accusation are liars in the sight of Allah.

questions.text: What is the warning for those who like that immorality should spread among the believers?
questions.answer: They will have a painful punishment in this world and the Hereafter.

questions.text: What was Abu Bakr's (RA) reaction toward the relative who participated in the slander, and what was Allah's advice?
questions.answer: He swore not to give to him anymore, but Allah advised: 'Let them pardon and overlook. Would you not like that Allah should forgive you?'

questions.text: What will happen to the disbelievers' own bodies on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: Their tongues, their hands, and their feet will testify against them as to what they used to do.

questions.text: What is the general rule mentioned regarding 'evil' vs 'good' people and words?
questions.answer: Evil words/women are for evil men, and good words/women are for good men.

questions.text: What is the etiquette of entering homes other than your own?
questions.answer: Do not enter until you have asked permission (Istinas) and greeted the inhabitants with 'Salam'.

questions.text: What should one do if told to 'go back' at someone's door?
questions.answer: Go back, for it is purer for you; and Allah is Knowing of what you do.

questions.text: Is it allowed to enter uninhabited houses where one has property or business?
questions.answer: Yes, there is no blame for entering such houses where there is a provision for you.

questions.text: What is the command to believing men regarding their gaze and chastity?
questions.answer: To lower their gaze and guard their private parts; that is purer for them.

questions.text: What is the command to believing women regarding their gaze and adornment?
questions.answer: To lower their gaze, guard their private parts, and not display their adornment except what is apparent.

questions.text: What is the 'Khimar' mentioned in the context of women's dress?
questions.answer: A headcovering that women should wrap over their chests (Juyub).

questions.text: Who are the relatives before whom a woman is allowed to display her hidden adornment?
questions.answer: Husbands, fathers, fathers-in-law, sons, step-sons, brothers, brothers' sons, sisters' sons, other women, or dependents.

questions.text: What is the advice regarding the marriage of single people and righteous servants?
questions.answer: To marry them off; if they are poor, Allah will enrich them from His bounty.

questions.text: What should those do who find no means for marriage?
questions.answer: They should keep themselves chaste until Allah enriches them from His bounty.

questions.text: What is the instruction regarding slaves who want a contract of freedom (Mukatabah)?
questions.answer: To write it for them if you know any good in them and give them from the wealth of Allah.

questions.text: Describe the famous 'Ayah of Light' (Ayat al-Nur).
questions.answer: Allah is the Light of the heavens and earth. His light is like a niche containing a lamp in a glass, the glass like a pearly star, lit from a blessed olive tree.

questions.text: How is the 'Blessed Olive Tree' described in the Ayah of Light?
questions.answer: As being neither of the east nor of the west, whose oil would almost glow even if untouched by fire—Light upon Light.

questions.text: What does Allah say about the 'houses' where His name is mentioned?
questions.answer: They are houses which Allah has permitted to be raised and where His name is celebrated, with people exalting Him morning and afternoon.

questions.text: Who are the 'men' whom commerce or sale does not distract from Allah's remembrance?
questions.answer: Those who establish prayer and give Zakat, fearing a Day when hearts and eyes will be overturned.

questions.text: What do the deeds of disbelievers resemble in this Surah?
questions.answer: A mirage in a lowland which a thirsty person thinks is water until he reaches it and finds nothing.

questions.text: Describe the 'darkness' parable for the state of the disbelievers.
questions.answer: Like darkness in a deep sea, covered by waves upon waves, covered by clouds—darkness upon darkness.

questions.text: Do the birds in the sky praise Allah?
questions.answer: Yes, 'the birds [with wings] outspread—each knows its prayer and its way of exalting Him.'

questions.text: How does Allah describe His power over the clouds and hail?
questions.answer: He drives the clouds, joins them, and makes them into a heap; and He sends down hail from mountains in the sky.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the diversity of movement in animals?
questions.answer: Allah created every creature from water; some crawl on their bellies, some walk on two legs, and some walk on four.

questions.text: What is the reaction of the hypocrites when called to the judgment of Allah and His Messenger?
questions.answer: If the judgment is in their favor, they come to him submissively; but if it is not, they turn away in aversion.

questions.text: What is the only response of the true believers when called to Allah and His Messenger for judgment?
questions.answer: They say, 'We hear and we obey (Sami'na wa ata'na).'

questions.text: What is the 'Istikhlaf' promise given to those who believe and do righteous deeds?
questions.answer: Allah will surely grant them succession (authority) upon the earth and establish for them their religion which He has approved for them.

questions.text: What will Allah replace the believers' fear with after they gain authority?
questions.answer: He will replace their fear with security, as long as they worship Him alone and associate nothing with Him.

questions.text: What are the 'three times of privacy' when even children and servants must ask permission to enter a room?
questions.answer: Before the Fajr prayer, when you take off your clothes at noon, and after the Isha prayer.

questions.text: Is there a different rule for 'elderly women' who no longer desire marriage regarding their outer garments?
questions.answer: Yes, there is no blame on them for putting aside their outer garments as long as they do not display their adornment; but to remain modest is better.

questions.text: Is there any blame for the blind, the lame, or the sick regarding eating at others' houses?
questions.answer: No, there is no restriction on them eating from their own houses or houses of relatives and friends, whether together or separately.

questions.text: What should believers do when they enter any house according to the conclusion of the Surah?
questions.answer: They should greet one another with a greeting from Allah (Salam), blessed and good.

questions.text: How should the believers act when they are with the Messenger on a 'matter of common interest'?
questions.answer: They should not depart until they have asked his permission.

questions.text: How should the believers address the Messenger of Allah?
questions.answer: They should not make the calling of the Messenger among them like the calling of one of them to another (they must show him proper respect).

questions.text: What is the warning for those who secretly slip away from the Prophet's assembly?
questions.answer: They are warned that a fitnah (trial) or a painful punishment might afflict them.

questions.text: To whom belongs everything in the heavens and the earth?
questions.answer: To Allah; He knows what state you are in and everything will return to Him.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the name 'An-Nur'?
questions.answer: The Light.

questions.text: What is the 'Pearly Star' comparison used for?
questions.answer: To describe the brilliance and purity of the glass containing the lamp of divine light.

questions.text: What is the 'Niche' (Mishkat) mentioned in the Ayah of Light?
questions.answer: A small recessed area in a wall used to place a lamp, concentrating its light.

questions.text: What is the 'display of adornment' (Tabarruj)?
questions.answer: Displaying one's beauty or ornaments in a way that attracts forbidden attention.

questions.text: What are the 'clear signs' (Ayatin Mubayyinatin) mentioned throughout the Surah?
questions.answer: The laws and parables revealed to guide the community.

questions.text: What is the 'success' (Falah) mentioned for those who obey Allah and the Messenger?
questions.answer: They are the ones who will be successful in both worlds.

questions.text: What is the 'Day hearts and eyes will be overturned'?
questions.answer: The Day of Resurrection.

questions.text: What is the 'dust' (Haba'an) mentioned in other Surahs, and how does it relate to the 'mirage' here?
questions.answer: Both describe the worthlessness of the deeds of disbelievers on the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What is the 'Light upon Light' (Nurun ala Nur)?
questions.answer: The combination of divine guidance (the Quran) and the natural light of faith in the believer's heart.

questions.text: What is the 'Istinas' (seeking permission)?
questions.answer: The social etiquette of making one's presence known and waiting for approval before entering a home.

questions.text: What is the 'Lowering of the Gaze' (Ghad al-Basar)?
questions.answer: A physical and spiritual practice of modesty to avoid forbidden looks.

questions.text: What is the 'Guard of private parts'?
questions.answer: Protecting oneself from all forms of illegal sexual activity.

questions.text: What is the 'Apparent adornment'?
questions.answer: That which is necessary to show, like the outer clothing or the face and hands according to some interpretations.

questions.text: What is the 'succession' (Istikhlaf)?
questions.answer: Being made leaders and inheritors of the land by Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'fitnah' warned against for those who disobey the Prophet?
questions.answer: A trial of faith, social chaos, or a severe worldly disaster.

questions.text: What is the 'Account' (Hisab) of Allah for the disbelievers?
questions.answer: A swift and precise judgment for their rejection of the truth.

questions.text: What is the 'Sign' in the alternating of night and day?
questions.answer: A lesson for those who have vision/insight.

questions.text: What is the 'Provision' (Rizq) mentioned for the believers in the end?
questions.answer: A noble and generous reward in Paradise.

questions.text: What is the 'Authority' (Sultan) mentioned regarding those who invoke others?
questions.answer: A valid proof or evidence, which they do not have.

questions.text: What is the 'Common interest' (Amrin Jami)?
questions.answer: Collective matters of the community like Friday prayer, war, or consultation.

questions.text: What is the 'Blessed Greeting'?
questions.answer: Assalamu Alaikum.

questions.text: What is the 'Record' (Kitab) which Allah mentions in the context of slaves?
questions.answer: The written agreement for emancipation.

questions.text: Why does Allah mention 'Mount Sinai' earlier in the Quran and the 'Olive Tree' here?
questions.answer: To connect the sources of physical and spiritual light.

questions.text: What is the 'lowland' (Qi'ah) in the mirage parable?
questions.answer: The deceptive place where the disbeliever expects to find his deeds rewarded.

questions.text: What are the 'Deep Seas' (Bahrun Lujjiyyun) in the darkness parable?
questions.answer: The vast, deep ocean where light cannot penetrate.

questions.text: What is the 'Glow' of the oil?
questions.answer: The purity and high quality of the source of the light.

questions.text: What is the 'Niche' (Mishkat)?
questions.answer: A physical symbol for the human heart containing the lamp of faith.

questions.text: Why does Allah mention 'hail' and 'lightning'?
questions.answer: As demonstrations of His control over powerful and even dangerous natural forces.

questions.text: What is the 'Submission' of animals?
questions.answer: Their total obedience to the instincts and physical laws Allah created for them.

questions.text: What is the 'Judgment of the Prophet'?
questions.answer: Deciding disputes between people based on divine revelation.

questions.text: What is the 'established' religion?
questions.answer: Islam, made firm and clear by Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Final Return'?
questions.answer: The resurrection and meeting with Allah for accountability.
